title: podman history command does not recognize {{.CreatedAt}}
body:
### Issue Description

I use ADO `Docker@2` task, but in reality my docker is just a symbolic link to podman.
When the docker task pushes the image it runs the following commands (taken from the build log):
```
/usr/bin/docker images
/usr/bin/docker push app505deploycr.azurecr.io/chip/toolbox:1.0.27
REPOSITORY                              TAG                 IMAGE ID      CREATED        SIZE
app505deploycr.azurecr.io/chip/toolbox  1.0.27              cb9346859f25  5 minutes ago  32.1 MB
app505deploycr.azurecr.io/chip/toolbox  1.0.26              cb9346859f25  5 minutes ago  32.1 MB
app505deploycr.azurecr.io/chip/toolbox  1.0.25              cb9346859f25  5 minutes ago  32.1 MB
app505deploycr.azurecr.io/chip/toolbox  latest              cb9346859f25  5 minutes ago  32.1 MB
docker.io/library/nginx                 1.23.3-alpine-slim  c59097225492  3 weeks ago    12.7 MB
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:f1bee861c2ba8de7590cbc91799d2bb8a3579bcf1d1519c2445bd2d385338787

Copying blob sha256:c4d67a5827ca405b493275f4e9df7d5e97f78ecb4bf48d77299e1175cb1dbcc4
Copying blob sha256:5e59460a18a391d945829d29a9b295b1c2fdeb0d09d1764012736698cacbdcc2
Copying blob sha256:4d614951f4fb3dc7b815073e2a2cc412134172bf9a3491000dc1cec15b60f166
Copying blob sha256:978f96cf0666a36fca3a6d0ff2b2d66de314eb4efd258fd283169d4dd0bd8d32
Copying blob sha256:817908ec6e361538f781bac22bfe581db59c2b718bc094724e37858a59660a55
Copying config sha256:cb9346859f25c555fbdf9be0aa73c776abad85c44f953e58e973aa7ce030cf66
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
/usr/bin/docker history --format createdAt:{{.CreatedAt}}; layerSize:{{.Size}}; createdBy:{{.CreatedBy}}; layerId:{{.ID}} --no-trunc app505deploycr.azurecr.io/chip/toolbox:1.0.27
/usr/bin/docker push app505deploycr.azurecr.io/chip/toolbox:latest
Getting image source signatures
createdAt:Error: template: history:1:23: executing "history" at <.CreatedAt>: can't evaluate field CreatedAt in type images.historyReporter
##[error]Error: template: history:1:23: executing "history" at <.CreatedAt>: can't evaluate field CreatedAt in type images.historyReporter
Copying blob sha256:c4d67a5827ca405b493275f4e9df7d5e97f78ecb4bf48d77299e1175cb1dbcc4
Copying blob sha256:5e59460a18a391d945829d29a9b295b1c2fdeb0d09d1764012736698cacbdcc2
Copying blob sha256:7cd52847ad775a5ddc4b58326cf884beee34544296402c6292ed76474c686d39
Copying blob sha256:f1bee861c2ba8de7590cbc91799d2bb8a3579bcf1d1519c2445bd2d385338787
Copying blob sha256:152a948bab3b2e19f92a88342da9b5271b1e2879272ecdad2200eb59159ea624
Copying blob sha256:d8a5a02a8c2d1e625a89a4956f6773baad42859509fb06b6c2e53b0ee3576dfd
Copying blob sha256:4d614951f4fb3dc7b815073e2a2cc412134172bf9a3491000dc1cec15b60f166
Copying blob sha256:817908ec6e361538f781bac22bfe581db59c2b718bc094724e37858a59660a55
Copying blob sha256:978f96cf0666a36fca3a6d0ff2b2d66de314eb4efd258fd283169d4dd0bd8d32
Copying config sha256:cb9346859f25c555fbdf9be0aa73c776abad85c44f953e58e973aa7ce030cf66
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
/usr/bin/docker history --format createdAt:{{.CreatedAt}}; layerSize:{{.Size}}; createdBy:{{.CreatedBy}}; layerId:{{.ID}} --no-trunc app505deploycr.azurecr.io/chip/toolbox:latest
createdAt:Error: template: history:1:23: executing "history" at <.CreatedAt>: can't evaluate field CreatedAt in type images.historyReporter
##[error]Error: template: history:1:23: executing "history" at <.CreatedAt>: can't evaluate field CreatedAt in type images.historyReporter
Finishing: push image
```
It seems that podman does not recognize `{{.CreatedAt}}`

### Steps to reproduce the issue

I reproduce it easily because I have a small pipeline that builds an image using the `Docker@2` ADO task and then pushes it using the same task.
But I think the following commands reproduce the issue:
```
/usr/bin/docker images
/usr/bin/docker push app505deploycr.azurecr.io/chip/toolbox:1.0.27
/usr/bin/docker history --format createdAt:{{.CreatedAt}}; layerSize:{{.Size}}; createdBy:{{.CreatedBy}}; layerId:{{.ID}} --no-trunc app505deploycr.azurecr.io/chip/toolbox:1.0.27
/usr/bin/docker push app505deploycr.azurecr.io/chip/toolbox:latest
```
where docker is a symbolic link to podman.

This is how I install podman in the pipeline:
```
      sudo apt-get update -qq -y
      sudo apt-get -qq -y install curl wget gnupg2

      source /etc/os-release
      echo deb http://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_${VERSION_ID}/ / | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.list

      wget -nv https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable/xUbuntu_${VERSION_ID}/Release.key -O- | sudo apt-key add -

      sudo apt-get update -qq -y
      sudo apt-get -qq -y install podman

      sudo ln -fs podman /usr/bin/docker
```

### Describe the results you received

podman outputs the following error messages:
```
Error: template: history:1:23: executing "history" at <.CreatedAt>: can't evaluate field CreatedAt in type images.historyReporter
```
But it does not abort the `Docker@2` task and so the image is pushed at the end. But the error message is surfaced in the build summary page and is unnecessary noise.

### Describe the results you expected

No error messages

### podman info output


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

It is entirely possible I am not using the latest version of podman. But that would be because I am unaware of how to install the latest version on build agent using Ubuntu package manager.

Maybe a solution is to use a newer package, but I do not know where to find it.

comments.body:
It seems to be  a duplicate of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14012
which has a fix in podman version  __v4.2.0-rc1__ (and later).

Edit 1:
I edited this comment and removed my irrelevant remark about Docker compatibility. 
~I noticed that the output from Podman and Docker is not identical. Here is an example with 
alpine:3.17.2~
Please open an issue on the other Docker incopatibilty.
